passage.title: Museum Timeline Redesign
passage.text: The history corner team wanted younger students to understand a story timeline more easily. At first, the team used one long timeline filled with tiny dates and notes, but students could not tell which event came first. A first-grade visitor group said "I can see the pictures, but I do not know where the story starts." After hearing that, the team split the timeline into short event cards and matched each event with one simple picture symbol. They also set a quick-order board near the entrance so students could stop and rebuild the event order in their minds. On the next test day, more students retold the timeline in the correct order after the next visit. The team noticed that short event cards helped more than long fact boxes. Because of that result, the museum planned to redesign more history walls with the same sequence system.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Improving a history timeline so younger students can follow it
Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
Students could not tell which event came first
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the team place near the entrance?
choices: Short signs
Color blocks
A quick-order board
Larger arrows
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does sequence mean in the seventh sentence?
choices: A person who leads the school
The order in which things happen
A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: The redesign made the event order easier to see and remember
It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
Use the same sequence system on more history displays
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
